# Vox Programming Language — Complete LLM / Agent Documentation Index
> Vox is an AI-native full-stack programming language that compiles to Rust and TypeScript.
> It is designed first as a target for large language models, then for human developers.
> One .vox file compiles to a database schema, a type-safe server, and a live browser UI.

## Agent Policy Entry Points
- [AGENTS.md](https://vox-lang.org/AGENTS.md): Cross-tool policy for all coding agents (required reading)
- [GEMINI.md](https://vox-lang.org/GEMINI.md): Antigravity-specific overlay for this repo
- [CLAUDE.md](https://vox-lang.org/CLAUDE.md): Claude Code overlay

## Core Documentation
- [Getting Started](https://vox-lang.org/tutorials/tut-getting-started/)
- [Language Syntax Reference](https://vox-lang.org/reference/ref-syntax/)
- [Decorators Reference](https://vox-lang.org/reference/ref-decorators/)
- [Type System Reference](https://vox-lang.org/reference/ref-type-system/)
- [Standard Library](https://vox-lang.org/reference/ref-stdlib/)
- [CLI Reference](https://vox-lang.org/reference/cli/)
- [Environment Variables SSOT](https://vox-lang.org/reference/env-vars/)
- [Clavis (Secret Management)](https://vox-lang.org/reference/clavis-ssot/)
- [MCP Tool Reference](https://vox-lang.org/reference/mcp-tool-reference/)

- [Architecture Index](https://vox-lang.org/architecture/architecture-index/)

- [Continuation Prompt Engineering](https://vox-lang.org/contributors/continuation-prompt-engineering/)

## Retired Surfaces (Do Not Use)
The following symbols are retired and must NOT be used in generated code:
- `vox-dei` → replaced by `vox-orchestrator`
- `vox-ars` → replaced by `vox-skills`
- `@component fn Name()` → replaced by `component Name() {}`
- `recall()` → replaced by `recall_async(query_spec)`
- `TURSO_URL` → replaced by `VOX_DB_URL`

*(This file is auto-maintained. The shorter /llms.txt is the primary lightweight discovery method.)*
